content: Precision oncology tailors cancer treatment based on individual tumor molecular characteristics rather than tissue of origin. Genomic profiling approaches: targeted gene panels (50-500+ genes, most common clinical use); whole exome sequencing (all coding regions, ~20,000 genes); whole genome sequencing (entire genome including non-coding); RNA sequencing (gene expression, fusions). Types of alterations detected: single nucleotide variants (SNVs), insertions/deletions (indels), copy number alterations (amplifications, deletions), gene fusions/rearrangements, tumor mutational burden (TMB), microsatellite instability (MSI). Actionable alterations have matched therapies with clinical evidence. Tumor-agnostic approvals: pembrolizumab for MSI-H/dMMR tumors (2017) and TMB-H (≥10 mut/Mb, 2019); larotrectinib/entrectinib for NTRK fusion-positive solid tumors (2018/2019); dabrafenib + trametinib for BRAF V600E solid tumors (2022). Challenges: variant interpretation (pathogenic vs VUS), tumor heterogeneity, clonal evolution, access/equity.
